w:r.w:t: It is our belief that we, as human beings, are custodians of all the available resources at our disposal, as provided for by providence, for the use and benefit of all mankind, regardless of creed, color, social status, or political affiliations.

Bearing in mind the fact that no life has more value than the other, circumstances of one’s birth and other vicissitudes of life largely determine how far one goes in this world. The Layeni Ogunmakinwa Memorial Foundation
, established by the children of Late Chief Dr. N.O.I. Ogunmakinwa,
good quality of life through healthy living awareness,
providing social welfare services as well as initiating steps within the public sector that can engender personnel productivity, thereby stimulating a culture of profound accountability.

We want to ensure that all people, especially those with little or no resources of their own, have access to quality education to alleviate poverty towards the goal of living a successful and meaningful life.
The Foundation presently has five areas of focus:
The Layeni Ogunmakinwa Memorial Diabetes Initiative;
The Motherless Babies Welfare Initiative; and
The Civil Service Merit Award Program.
The Layeni Ogunmakinwa Memorial Diabetes Initiative
is designed to disseminate information about the scourge of Diabetes, provide avenues for status identification through the establishment
of testing centers as well as partner with both healthcare and nutrition agencies in the areas of care and proper dietary regime.

provides monthly allowance, based on a minimum cost of living index approach to widows in the community. It is a program we hope to expand from the current thirty-three beneficiaries as funds become more available to benefit other needy members of the society.
is aimed at funding the education of the less privileged in society. We shall partner with various willing private sector stakeholders as well as institutions of learning from primary to
level to create an avenue whereby
the corporate responsibilities of the organizations could be leveraged for the benefit of identified indigent students.
Initiative, as the name implies, caters for the welfare of the almost forgotten members of the larger society. It is our goal to make sure that they are not left behind in the scheme of things as

pertain to quality of life and social integration. We want to ensure that they are nurtured in a warm and caring environment devoid of systemic neglect and abuse by partnering with various government and non-governmental agencies charged with their welfare to promote better opportunities for a brighter future.
The Civil Service Merit Award Program

is geared towards the fundamentals of individual’s desire for Acknowledgement, Celebration and Encouragement. To this end, we shall work in partnership with the civil service as well as the Department of Establishment and Training to facilitate a process of identifying, encouraging, rewarding and possibly celebrating those public servants who stand high, way above their peers, naturally in the daily discharge of their duties as a means to encourage others to emulate the sterling qualities inherent in them towards the goal of bringing back the glory days of public service delivery.

At the Layeni Ogunmakinwa Memorial Foundation, we maintain that all individuals share a responsibility to manage the world’s resources, which are provided for the collective benefit of everyone, irrespective of creed, color, social status, or political affiliation.
Every life holds equal value, though circumstances of birth and fortune often shape individual opportunities.
Founded in honor of Chief Dr. N.O.I. Ogunmakinwa

, the Layeni Ogunmakinwa Memorial Foundation is dedicated to uplifting the vulnerable, empowering the underserved, and restoring hope where it is most needed. Our mission is to improve quality of life through health awareness, social welfare, and initiatives that foster accountability and productivity in the public sector. Above all, we strive to ensure that all people
have access to education and opportunities that alleviate poverty and enable meaningful, successful lives.
– Raising awareness, providing testing centers, and partnering with healthcare and nutrition agencies to fight the growing scourge of diabetes.
– Offering monthly stipends to widows based on cost-of-living needs. Currently supporting 33 women, we aim to expand this lifeline to many more

– Funding education for underprivileged students from primary to tertiary levels, in partnership with private sector stakeholders and institutions. Every scholarship breaks the cycle of poverty and opens doors to opportunity.
– Ensuring vulnerable children are not forgotten, by providing nurturing environments and brighter futures through collaboration with government and NGOs.
– Recognizing and celebrating outstanding public servants to inspire excellence,

weekly live broadcast aired every Wednesday at 10:00 a.m. on Fresh Radio 102.9FM. The program provides practical entrepreneurial tips and insights tailored to both startup founders and established business owners, offering guidance to help them grow, innovate, and succeed.
Every initiative is a step toward a society where dignity, opportunity, and hope are not privileges but rights. With your partnership
whether through donations, collaboration, or advocacy
we can expand these programs, protect the vulnerable, and build a future where no one is left behind.
Uplifting Lives. Restoring Hope. Building Futures.

At the Layeni Ogunmakinwa Memorial Foundation, we believe all individuals share a responsibility to steward the world’s resources for the collective good—regardless of creed, color, social status, or political affiliation.

Every life holds equal value, though circumstances of birth and fortune often shape opportunity. Founded in honor of Chief Dr. N.O.I. Ogunmakinwa by his children, our Foundation is dedicated to uplifting the vulnerable, empowering the underserved, and restoring hope where it is most needed.

Our mission is to improve quality of life through health awareness, social welfare, and initiatives that foster accountability and productivity in the public sector. Above all, we strive to ensure that all people—especially those with limited means—have access to education and opportunities that alleviate poverty and enable meaningful, successful lives.
We focus on six key initiatives:
– Raising awareness, providing testing centers, and partnering with healthcare and nutrition agencies to fight diabetes.
– Monthly stipends for widows based on cost
living needs. Currently supporting 33 women, with plans to expand.
– Funding education for underprivileged students from primary to tertiary levels. Every scholarship breaks the cycle of poverty.
– Providing care, protection, and brighter futures for vulnerable children through collaboration with government and NGOs.
– Recognizing outstanding public servants to inspire excellence, integrity, and renewed pride in public service delivery.
– A weekly live broadcast every Wednesday at 10:00 a.m. on Fresh Radio 102.9FM, offering entrepreneurial tips and insights for startups and established businesses.
Every initiative is a step toward a society where dignity, opportunity, and hope are not privileges but rights.
Every scholarship breaks the cycle of poverty.
Every stipend restores dignity to widows.
Every public servant honored inspires integrity.
we can expand these programs, protect the vulnerable, and build a future where no one is left behind.
“Together, we can ensure no one is left behind.”
Tagline in Playfair Display Italic with gold accent
Hero image: smiling children or hands joined together
Deep Blue background with Emerald Green accent line

At the Layeni Ogunmakinwa Memorial Foundation, we believe all individuals share a responsibility to steward the world’s resources for the collective good—regardless of creed, color, social status, or political affiliation.

Every life holds equal value, though circumstances of birth and fortune often shape opportunity. Founded in honor of Chief Dr. N.O.I. Ogunmakinwa by his children, our Foundation is dedicated to uplifting the vulnerable, empowering the underserved, and restoring hope where it is most needed.
– Raising awareness, providing testing centers, and partnering with healthcare and nutrition agencies.
– Monthly stipends for widows in need. Currently supporting 33 women, with plans to expand.
– Funding education for underprivileged students from primary to tertiary levels.
– Providing care, protection, and brighter futures for vulnerable children.
– Recognizing outstanding public servants to inspire excellence and accountability.
– Weekly live broadcast every Wednesday at 10:00 a.m. on Fresh Radio 102.9FM, offering entrepreneurial tips and insights.
Use icons in gold circles beside each initiative for quick visual recognition.
Every initiative is a step toward a society where dignity, opportunity, and hope are not privileges but rights.
we can expand these programs, protect the vulnerable, and build a future where no one is left behind.
Background: Soft Gray with gold accent line
Closing quote in Playfair Display Italic: “Together, we can ensure no one is left behind.”
